page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 137 GB NVMe SSD, offering low-latency local storage capabilities. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor operates at a base speed of 2.3 GHz and features a large 504 MB L3 cache, alongside 2 MB L2 and 48 KB L1d caches. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing. General CPU performance, including multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores, falls within the average range.

While the server exhibits strong performance in text-based processing and small-model LLM text generation, it presents certain resource limitations. Uncached large-block memory read bandwidth and PassMark memory scores are positioned in the lower tier, indicating potential bottlenecks for memory-intensive workloads that bypass the cache. However, the integrated 137 GB NVMe SSD provides fast local storage, making the instance suitable for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-8-lssd offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation and fast local scratch space without the overhead of external storage. It is well-suited for applications such as relational database hosting, web serving, text processing pipelines, and light machine learning inference using small models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 30.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance features a bundled 137 GB local NVMe SSD for low-latency storage, but lacks GPU acceleration. Its x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier capabilities in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing, alongside strong text generation speeds for small LLM models. However, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth and overall memory benchmarks represent a performance tradeoff, landing in the lower tier. This server type provides a balanced resource profile, making it qualitatively cost-effective for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources and fast local storage.

tagline: A dedicated eight-vCPU virtual machine featuring integrated local NVMe storage and advanced Intel matrix extensions for balanced general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs and 30 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor
Bundled 137 GB local NVMe SSD for low-latency storage operations
Supports Intel AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math
Top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing
Tradeoff in uncached large-block memory read bandwidth
Suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation
